agent_a.rationale: CT chest shows a 3.8 cm right hilar mass with mediastinal/hilar lymphadenopathy, highly concerning for primary lung malignancy and suspicious for SCLC.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Right hilar mass (3.8 cm) with ipsilateral mediastinal and hilar lymphadenopathy. Findings are highly concerning for a primary lung malignancy; central location and clinical context of SIADH raise high suspicion for small-cell lung carcinoma.

CT chest (2025-08-04) revealed right hilar mass concerning for SCLC as paraneoplastic etiology. EBUS-TBNA (2025-08-19) confirmed small-cell lung carcinoma. PET/CT (2025-08-28): FDG-avid right hilar mass (SUVmax 12.4) and ipsilateral mediastinal nodes; no distant metastases. MRI brain negative. Staging: LS-SCLC.
